seo.title: Flowise Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Flowise agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using Flowise for AI-driven document review, case research, and client advisory chatflows face heightened risks due to strict attorney-client privilege, bar ethics rules, and court standards. Unvalidated LLM outputs in drag-and-drop chains can lead to hallucinated citations or privilege breaches, as seen in the Mata v. Avianca case. Vex provides runtime compliance guardrails to ensure verifiable, privilege-safe responses before they reach clients or filings.

complianceRequirements.description: All outputs must redact or block privileged client information; no storage or external transmission allowed
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: PII/privilege entity detection with auto-redaction and block policies on chatflow outputs

complianceRequirements.regulation: Bar association ethics rules (ABA Model Rule 1.1)
complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content requires licensed attorney review; cannot be filed without verification
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Watermarking and review-required flags on all legal outputs with audit trails

complianceRequirements.description: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinations prohibited
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Real-time case law verification against legal databases with hallucination scoring

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information cannot be used for model training or external APIs
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Data isolation policies preventing training data leakage with zero-retention guarantees

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Chatflows generating fake case law references leading to court sanctions
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca (2023) - $45K sanctions for AI-generated nonexistent cases

industryRisks.name: Privilege disclosure in RAG retrieval
industryRisks.description: Vector stores surfacing privileged docs in responses
industryRisks.example: Client strategy docs exposed in opposing counsel research query

industryRisks.name: Unreviewed AI advice filed directly
industryRisks.description: Attorney accepts unverified chatflow output for motions
industryRisks.example: Malpractice claim after AI-suggested statute misapplied

industryRisks.name: Context loss in multi-agent handoff
industryRisks.description: Sequential agents dropping privilege flags between research/review stages
industryRisks.example: Research agent flags privilege, review agent files anyway

implementation.title: Add Vex Callback to Flowise Chatflow
implementation.description: Integrate Vex as a post-chain callback handler to validate all outputs against legal policies

implementation.title: Vector Store Retrieval Guardrails
implementation.description: Block low-relevance or privileged document retrieval with semantic relevance scoring

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Context Preservation
implementation.description: Ensure privilege flags and citations propagate across agent handoffs

caseStudyScenario.heading: Flowise in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized plaintiff's firm deploys Flowise to accelerate personal injury case research. Their chatflow chains a vector store of 50K+ case documents with a research agent, citation validator, and brief generator. Paralegals query 'precedent for slip-and-fall in grocery stores,' expecting verified cases for motions. However, the RAG retrieval pulls low-relevance privileged strategy docs from past cases, the citation node hallucinates 'Smith v. Safeway (2022)' which doesn't exist, and the multi-agent handoff drops the privilege flag before brief generation.

Without guardrails, the attorney files the brief with fake citations and accidentally discloses opposing counsel's settlement strategy from retrieved docs. The defense moves for sanctions citing Mata v. Avianca, risking $100K+ in fees and case dismissal.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex callbacks integrated, the vector guardrail blocks privileged docs (relevance <0.75), citation verification flags the hallucinated Smith case against Westlaw/Lexis APIs, and agent handoff validation forces attorney review. The chatflow returns 'No compliant citations found - manual research required,' preventing filing. The firm avoids sanctions, implements firm-wide Vex policies, and reduces research time 40% with compliant automation. Audit logs provide malpractice defense documentation.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex guardrails to your Flowise legal chatflows today to prevent hallucinations, protect privilege, and ensure bar compliance.
